config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan “kepentingan masyarakat,” karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kekuasaan mudah disalahgunakan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan dasar orang per orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan tanggung jawab sosial berjalan seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should automatically come before individual rights. From my perspective in China, I have seen that appeals to the “greater good” can be used to justify too much control, and once individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes easy to ignore abuse or silence dissent. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights should be absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and social order do matter. The real standard should be that society can limit individual rights only in a narrow, transparent, and accountable way, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak individu yang menjadi pelindung dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang proporsional, transparan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dengan menganggap kebebasan dasar orang sebagai hal sekunder. Bagi saya, masyarakat kuat bukan yang paling mudah menekan individu, melainkan yang paling mampu menjaga keseimbangan antara kepentingan bersama dan martabat tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have automatic priority over individual rights, because that gives too much room for abuse. From my perspective in China, I have seen how easily “social interests” can be used as a broad excuse to silence criticism or pressure people into obedience, even when the harm to individuals is real. That said, I also do not support a rigid individualism that ignores public health, safety, or basic social order; those are legitimate concerns. For me, the better principle is that individual rights should be the default, and any restriction should be narrow, clearly justified, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap warga. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi itu harus bersifat sangat terbatas, jelas dasarnya, dan bisa diuji akuntabilitasnya, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa dan pembungkaman kritik dengan dalih kebaikan bersama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling kuat menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengurus kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I have seen how quickly “the public interest” can become a convenient label for overreach, and once that happens, ordinary people have little protection against abuse or forced obedience. Public health, safety, and social order do matter, but they do not justify making individual rights secondary by default. A society is stronger when it protects rights first and then limits them only in narrow, transparent, and accountable cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
